text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. They involve team members carefully examining each other’s code before it is added to the main project. One key benefit is catching bugs early. When someone else reads your code, they often spot mistakes or logic problems that you missed. This saves time and money later. Another advantage is knowledge sharing. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, and senior developers also gain fresh perspectives. Finally, code reviews help improve overall team quality. They encourage clear, well-structured code and promote consistent standards across the project.
